chapter_summary: John tells us that Jesus — called 'the Word' — was with God before anything was made and is God Himself. Jesus came into the world as a real person, but many did not recognize Him. John the Baptist pointed to Jesus as the Lamb of God. The first disciples heard about Jesus and began to follow Him.
main_lesson: Jesus, God's own Son, came to live with us as a person so we could know and love God.

memory_verse.kjv: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus — God's own Word — became a real person and lived among us. He was full of kindness and truth, and we could see how wonderful God truly is.

parent_guide: John 1 opens with some of the most profound theology in the Bible. John uses 'the Word' (Greek: Logos) to describe Jesus — a title that would have meant 'divine reason and communication' to Greek-speaking readers, and 'God's spoken creative power' to Jewish readers. For children, the key idea is simple and breathtaking: Jesus always existed with God, was there when everything was made, and then became a real human being so we could know God personally. Emphasise that Jesus is not a created being — He is the Creator. The calling of the first disciples shows us that Jesus invites everyone personally: 'Come and see.'
application_for_children: Jesus was not just a good teacher who lived a long time ago — He is the eternal Son of God who became a person because He loves you. You can come to Him and He will welcome you.

verses.kjv: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
verses.little_bible: Before anything was made — before the world, before the stars — Jesus was already there. He was with God, and He was God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Before everything began, Jesus was already there with God.
verses.meaning: John opens by echoing Genesis. Jesus ('the Word') is eternal and divine — not created, but God Himself. He existed before time began.
verses.memory_phrase: In the beginning was Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Jesus has always been with You and has always been real. That amazes me!
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus was there before anything was made, what does that tell you about how powerful He is?
verses.family_discussion: John calls Jesus 'the Word.' Just like words carry a message, Jesus is how God shows and says everything about Himself. How has God spoken to your family through Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Look up at the sky tonight and remember: Jesus was there before even the stars were made.
verses.illustration_prompt: A vast, dark, starless universe — pure deep blue-black — with a single warm golden light radiating from the centre. Simple, majestic, awe-inspiring. Watercolour style.

verses.kjv: The same was in the beginning with God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus was with God right from the very start. They have always been together.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was with God from the very beginning.
verses.meaning: John repeats the truth to anchor it: Jesus was not created at some later point. He has been with God from eternity.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus has always been with God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have always been with God. You never had a beginning. That is so big and wonderful!
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when YOU had a beginning — like your birthday? Why is it special that Jesus never had a beginning?
verses.family_discussion: We are created beings — we had a start. Jesus is eternal — He had no start. How does knowing Jesus is eternal make you trust Him more?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today one fact about Jesus that amazes you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures of radiant light side by side in a timeless golden space — no horizon, no sky, just warmth and togetherness. Soft watercolour glow.

verses.kjv: All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
verses.little_bible: Jesus made everything! Every star, every tree, every creature — nothing that exists was made without Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus made everything — every animal, every star, every flower.
verses.meaning: Jesus is not merely a created being — He is the Creator. Every single created thing owes its existence to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus made everything.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You made the whole world! Thank You for making ME. I am glad You are the Creator.
verses.discussion_question: Look around you right now. Can you name five things Jesus made?
verses.family_discussion: If Jesus made everything, how should that change the way we treat the world He made? How should it change the way we treat each other?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and pick one thing in nature — a leaf, a stone, a cloud — and thank Jesus for making it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child's hands holding a small glowing globe covered in tiny animals, trees, mountains, and oceans. Above the globe, rays of warm light pour down. Joyful watercolour palette.

verses.kjv: In him was life; and the life was the light of men.
verses.little_bible: Jesus holds life inside Himself — real life. And that life shines like a light to help every person find their way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus has real life and shines like a light for everyone.
verses.meaning: Jesus is the source of all life — physical and spiritual. His light is the truth and salvation that guides every human being.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is life and light.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the light of my life. Shine on me today so I can find my way.
verses.discussion_question: What does a light do in a dark room? How is Jesus like that light in our lives?
verses.family_discussion: The Bible says Jesus is the light of people. As a family, where do you most need His light right now — at school, at home, in your hearts?
verses.do_it_today: Turn on a light in a dark room today and say: 'Jesus, You are my light. Thank You.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A lantern resting on a rocky path at night, casting a warm circle of golden light on wildflowers and stones around it. Stars above, soft watercolour darkness surrounding the glow.

verses.kjv: And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.
verses.little_bible: Jesus's light keeps shining, even in the darkest places. But the darkness could not put it out and did not understand it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus's light shines in the dark and the dark cannot put it out.
verses.meaning: Darkness — sin, evil, unbelief — cannot overcome or extinguish Jesus. His light is stronger. Yet many did not receive or recognise it.
verses.memory_phrase: The light shines in darkness.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when things feel dark and scary, remind me that Your light is shining and nothing can put it out.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been somewhere really dark? What happened when someone turned on even a tiny light?
verses.family_discussion: The darkness could not understand or stop the light. What are some 'dark things' in our world today, and how does Jesus's light address them?
verses.do_it_today: If you feel worried or afraid today, whisper: 'Jesus, Your light is shining. I am not afraid.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A single candle flame burning steadily in the middle of deep darkness. The flame is strong and unwavering, casting warm light on a child's face peering in with wonder. Watercolour style.

verses.kjv: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
verses.little_bible: God sent a special man to prepare the way for Jesus. His name was John.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God sent a man named John to get people ready for Jesus.
verses.meaning: John the Baptist had a God-given mission — not to be the light himself, but to point people toward the Light. God sends people with a purpose.
verses.memory_phrase: God sent John with a mission.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for sending people to tell others about Jesus. Help me be someone like that too.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God sent someone to get people ready before Jesus came?
verses.family_discussion: God called John for a specific job. Has God ever given you or someone in your family a special mission or calling? How did you know?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person you could tell about Jesus this week — just like John told people about the Light.
verses.illustration_prompt: A rugged but kind man in rough clothing standing in a sun-lit wilderness, one hand raised and pointing toward the horizon where a warm glow is coming. Desert wildflowers around his feet. Watercolour style.

verses.kjv: The same came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all men through him might believe.
verses.little_bible: John came to tell people about Jesus — the Light — so that everyone who heard him could believe.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John came to tell everyone about Jesus so they could believe.
verses.meaning: John's whole purpose was to testify — to point people to Jesus so they would believe. Witnessing is not about self-promotion but about directing others to Christ.
verses.memory_phrase: John pointed people to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me be a good witness like John — someone who points others to You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be a witness? Have you ever told someone something good about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: John's entire life purpose was to point to Jesus. What does your family's life point people toward? What do others see when they watch you?
verses.do_it_today: Say one true, good thing about Jesus to someone in your home today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man pointing enthusiastically toward a brilliant distant light on the horizon while a group of people with wide, curious eyes turn to look. Rolling hills, soft sunrise. Warm watercolour tones.

verses.kjv: He was not that Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
verses.little_bible: John was not the Light himself. His job was to tell people about the true Light — Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John was not the Light. He was sent to tell about the Light.
verses.meaning: John was a great man, but not the Messiah. He knew his role. Humility means knowing who we are and who Jesus is.
verses.memory_phrase: John was not the Light.
verses.prayer: God, help me remember that Jesus is the special one — not me. Help me point to Him, not myself.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important that John knew he was NOT the Light? What would have happened if he thought he was?
verses.family_discussion: It is easy to want to be the important one. How can your family practise pointing to Jesus instead of taking credit for yourselves?
verses.do_it_today: If you do something good today, quietly say thank You to Jesus for helping you — and give Him the credit.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man standing in shade, one arm extended, pointing to a blazing sun in the sky. His face is turned away from himself and toward the light. Simple, humble posture. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: That was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.
verses.little_bible: Jesus is the true Light — and He gives His light to every single person who is born into this world.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus is the true Light that shines on every person in the world.
verses.meaning: Jesus's light is universal — it reaches every person. No one is outside its reach. He is the only true light for all humanity.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the true Light for everyone.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that Your light is for me — and for every person in the world. Help people everywhere find You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus lights up every person. Do you think that means His love is for people in other countries too? Can you name a country and pray for them?
verses.family_discussion: The true light reaches every person on earth. How does your family's faith connect to God's heart for the whole world?
verses.do_it_today: Find a country on a map that you do not know well and say a short prayer: 'Jesus, let Your light shine there.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing figure standing on a globe, arms wide open, rays of soft golden light streaming out to every corner of the earth. Children of different backgrounds look up in wonder. Watercolour style.

verses.kjv: He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.
verses.little_bible: Jesus came and lived in the very world He had made — but most people did not recognise Him or know who He was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus made the world and came to live in it, but people did not know Him.
verses.meaning: The tragedy of the Incarnation: the Creator came to His own creation, and creation did not recognise Him. Sin blinds people to the truth.
verses.memory_phrase: His world did not know Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I am sorry that people did not recognise You. I want to know You and never forget who You are.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think Jesus felt when the people He made did not know who He was?
verses.family_discussion: People had the Scriptures and still missed Jesus. How do we make sure we do not 'know about' Jesus but fail to truly know Him?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw one thing you know is true about Jesus and stick it somewhere you will see it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking down a busy market street while people rush past him, eyes down, not noticing. He looks around with gentle sadness. Warm crowd colours, watercolour style.

verses.kjv: He came unto his own, and his own received him not.
verses.little_bible: Jesus came to His own people — the people He had chosen and loved for so long — but they turned Him away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came to His own people but they did not accept Him.
verses.meaning: The Jewish people had been prepared through centuries of prophecy to receive the Messiah. Most still rejected Him. Rejection is part of Jesus's story.
verses.memory_phrase: His own people turned Him away.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You came and were turned away. Thank You that You still came anyway, even knowing that. Thank You that You did not give up.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been left out or turned away by people you cared about? How do you think that felt for Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was rejected by His own people. Has your family ever been rejected for following Jesus? How did you handle it?
verses.do_it_today: If someone is left out today — in class, at play — include them. Remember how Jesus felt.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing at the door of a home, hand raised to knock, while faces peer through a window and the door stays shut. Evening light, quiet sadness on Jesus's face. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name:
verses.little_bible: But everyone who welcomed Jesus and believed in Him — He gave them the amazing gift of becoming children of God!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone who believes in Jesus becomes a child of God.
verses.meaning: Faith in Jesus brings the most extraordinary gift: adoption into God's family. Believing in His name means trusting in who He truly is.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in Jesus — become God's child.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I receive You! I believe in Your name. Thank You for making me a child of God.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to you that you can become a child of God? How does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Every member of your family who believes in Jesus is a child of God. How does being in God's family change the way you think about your identity?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'I am a child of God because I believe in Jesus.' Say it three times with confidence.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child being lifted up by gentle, large, glowing hands toward a warm sky. The child's face is full of joy and wonder. Soft clouds, golden light, watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Which were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
verses.little_bible: Being a child of God is not about your family name or where you were born. It comes from God Himself — He is the one who gives this new life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Being God's child is not about your family. God makes you His child.
verses.meaning: Spiritual birth — being born again — does not come from human ancestry, effort, or decision alone. It is a gift of God, sovereign and divine.
verses.memory_phrase: Born of God — not of people.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that being Your child is Your gift to me. I did not earn it. You gave it.
verses.discussion_question: You cannot choose your family you are born into. But you CAN choose to receive Jesus. What is special about that?
verses.family_discussion: Our earthly family is given to us. Our spiritual family comes through faith. How does your family talk about spiritual birth and what it means to be truly 'in' God's family?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for one thing about your family today — and also thank Him for making you part of His family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two images side by side: one a baby in a crib surrounded by a loving family; the other the same child surrounded by a heavenly glow with God's hand over them. Soft, gentle watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus — God's living Word — became a real, human person and came to live with us. We could see God's own glory in Him. He was full of kindness and truth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's Word became a real person. Jesus came to live with us, full of kindness and truth.
verses.meaning: The Incarnation — the most important event in history. God became human without ceasing to be God. He lived among us so we could see and know the Father.
verses.memory_phrase: The Word became flesh and lived with us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for leaving heaven and becoming a person just like me. You did that because You love me. I love You too.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think it was important for Jesus to become a real person and not just stay in heaven?
verses.family_discussion: God could have saved us without coming to earth — but He chose to come, to live with us, to be one of us. What does that tell you about God's heart?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of baby Jesus — and remember: that baby was God, coming to be with you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A baby lying in a manger made of rough wood, wrapped in simple cloth, but surrounded by a gentle golden glow. A star shines above. Animals nearby. Warm, tender watercolour style.

verses.kjv: John bare witness of him, and cried, saying, This was he of whom I spake, He that cometh after me is preferred before me: for he was before me.
verses.little_bible: John told everyone loudly: 'This is the one I was talking about! He came after me, but He is far greater than me — because He existed long before I was even born!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'Jesus is the one I told you about. He is greater than me!'
verses.meaning: John the Baptist publicly proclaimed Jesus's superiority. Though Jesus was younger in human years, He pre-existed John eternally. John rejoiced in pointing to One greater.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is greater — He was before me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are greater than everyone. I want to always honour You as the greatest.
verses.discussion_question: John was happy to say someone else was greater than him. Is it easy or hard to say that about someone else? Why?
verses.family_discussion: John did not compete with Jesus — he celebrated Him. How can your family practise celebrating each other rather than competing?
verses.do_it_today: Say something genuinely kind and celebratory about someone else today — focus all the attention on them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A strong, joyful man standing by a river, arms raised toward a distant figure of Jesus walking on the path. People around him turning to look. Bright daytime scene, blue river, green reeds. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And of his fulness have all we received, and grace for grace.
verses.little_bible: From Jesus's overflowing fullness, we have all received gifts — kindness upon kindness, blessing upon blessing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: From Jesus we receive kindness and blessing again and again.
verses.meaning: Jesus is inexhaustibly full of grace. Every believer draws from His abundant supply — and it never runs out. Grace piles upon grace.
verses.memory_phrase: Grace upon grace from Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that Your kindness never runs out. Every day I receive more of Your grace.
verses.discussion_question: What is something kind that Jesus has done for you? Can you think of more than one thing?
verses.family_discussion: Grace upon grace means God's kindness keeps coming. How have you seen God's grace keep showing up again and again in your family's life?
verses.do_it_today: Start a 'grace list' — write down three ways Jesus has been kind to you this week.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding a cup that overflows with golden light, with more light still pouring down from above. The child's face is delighted and the ground around them sparkles. Joyful watercolour style.

verses.kjv: For the law was given by Moses, but grace and truth came by Jesus Christ.
verses.little_bible: God gave Moses the law — rules to live by. But Jesus brought something better: grace and truth — His very own kindness and realness.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Moses brought God's rules. Jesus brought kindness and truth.
verses.meaning: The law reveals what is right but cannot save. Jesus brings grace — unearned favour — and truth — the full reality of God. He fulfils and surpasses the law.
verses.memory_phrase: Grace and truth came through Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You did not just give me rules — You gave me Yourself, full of grace and truth.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between following rules and being shown kindness? Which one do you prefer?
verses.family_discussion: The law tells us what we should do; grace tells us what Jesus has done for us. How does your family balance living by God's standards with resting in His grace?
verses.do_it_today: Show someone grace today — forgive something, let something go, be kind even when it is not deserved.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two scenes: one showing stone tablets with writing on a mountaintop (Moses, dramatic sky); the other showing Jesus holding out open hands filled with warm golden light. Side by side in watercolour.

verses.kjv: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him.
verses.little_bible: No one has ever been able to see God directly. But Jesus — God's one and only Son, who is closest to the Father's heart — has shown us exactly what God is like.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: No one has seen God. But Jesus shows us what God is like.
verses.meaning: Jesus is the perfect revelation of the Father. To know Jesus is to know God. He is the only one who can truly show us who God is.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus shows us what God is like.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I want to know what God is like, I look at You. Thank You for showing me the Father.
verses.discussion_question: If you want to know what God is like — what kind of person He is — where do you look?
verses.family_discussion: Everything Jesus said and did reveals the Father. Which story of Jesus in the Gospels best shows your family what God is like? Why that one?
verses.do_it_today: Read one story about Jesus today and ask: 'What does this show me about God the Father?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling down to gently speak to a small child, while above and behind Him a soft radiant presence — like warm, invisible sunlight — fills the sky. The child's eyes are wide with recognition. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And this is the record of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?
verses.little_bible: The religious leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites — temple workers — to go and ask John: 'Who are you, exactly?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Religious leaders came to John and asked: 'Who are you?'
verses.meaning: John's ministry was creating significant attention. The authorities needed to know if he was claiming to be the Messiah. John's response would define his identity publicly.
verses.memory_phrase: They came and asked: 'Who are you?'
verses.prayer: God, help me know who I am — a child of God — and to be honest about it like John was.
verses.discussion_question: If someone important came to you and asked 'Who are you?' what would you say?
verses.family_discussion: Our identity matters. John knew exactly who he was and who he was not. How does your family talk about identity — who you are in Christ versus what the world says about you?
verses.do_it_today: Finish this sentence out loud: 'I am __________ and I belong to God.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of neatly-robed priests and temple workers standing on a riverbank, looking carefully at a rugged, plain-dressed John. Serious faces, a clear contrast in appearance. Daylight watercolour scene.

verses.kjv: And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.
verses.little_bible: John was completely honest. He told them plainly: 'I am not the Christ — I am not the Messiah.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John was honest. He said: 'I am not the Christ.'
verses.meaning: John was humble and truthful. He could have claimed more, but he did not. Honest about who he was not — so he could clearly point to who Jesus is.
verses.memory_phrase: John said honestly: 'I am not the Christ.'
verses.prayer: God, help me be honest and humble like John — not pretending to be more than I am.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important to be honest about who you are, even if being someone more important might be exciting?
verses.family_discussion: Humility means being accurate about yourself — not putting yourself down, but not puffing yourself up either. How does your family practise honest humility?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind today without telling anyone about it. Let God see it — not people.
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing straight and calm, hands at his sides, speaking directly and confidently to a group of questioning officials. His expression is honest and peaceful. Bright daylight, open landscape. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And they asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou that prophet? And he answered, No.
verses.little_bible: They kept asking him: 'Are you Elijah, the great prophet?' 'No,' said John. 'Are you THE Prophet we are waiting for?' 'No,' he answered again.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They asked: 'Are you Elijah?' John said: 'No.' 'Are you the prophet?' 'No.'
verses.meaning: The questioners worked through the expected figures — Elijah, the Prophet like Moses. John denied them all. He had one identity: forerunner of Christ.
verses.memory_phrase: John kept saying: 'No, not me.'
verses.prayer: Jesus, I do not need to be someone special or famous. Help me be exactly who You made me to be.
verses.discussion_question: People kept asking John if he was someone important. Why do you think he kept saying no?
verses.family_discussion: We often want to be someone significant. John found contentment in his one calling: point to Jesus. What calling has God given your family — and are you content in it?
verses.do_it_today: Think about one gift or talent God has given you. How can you use it today to point others to Jesus?
verses.illustration_prompt: John shaking his head calmly while persistent questioners lean in with scrolls and quills, ready to write down his answer. A peaceful river scene behind him. Simple, warm watercolour.

verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?
verses.little_bible: They pressed him again: 'Then who ARE you? We need an answer to take back to the people who sent us. What do you say about yourself?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They asked again: 'Then who are you? Tell us!'
verses.meaning: The questioners pushed for a clear answer. They needed a definitive identity. John's response would come from Scripture — his identity was defined by God's Word.
verses.memory_phrase: What do you say about yourself?
verses.prayer: God, I want my answer about who I am to come from what YOU say about me — not what others think.
verses.discussion_question: What does God say about who you are? Can you think of one thing?
verses.family_discussion: The world often asks us to define ourselves. What does your family say when the world asks: 'Who are you?' Do you answer from the world's definition or God's?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing God says about you in the Bible. Start with 'I am...'
verses.illustration_prompt: A circle of serious faces pressing in around John with scrolls and quill pens raised, ready to record. John stands calm and unhurried in the middle. Jordan riverbank, watercolour midday light.

verses.kjv: He said, I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, as said the prophet Esaias.
verses.little_bible: John answered them with words from the prophet Isaiah: 'I am a voice calling out in the desert: Get the path ready for the Lord!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I am a voice saying: Get the road ready for the Lord!'
verses.meaning: John defined himself by his function, not his status. He was a voice — not the message, but the one who carried it. His purpose was preparation.
verses.memory_phrase: I am a voice for the Lord.
verses.prayer: God, make me a voice for You — someone who points others to Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: John said his job was to 'get the road ready' for Jesus. What do you think that means? How can you get your heart ready for Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: John's identity came from Scripture — Isaiah wrote about him 700 years before he was born. How does knowing God planned you before you were born change how you see yourself?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing to 'prepare the way' — tidy your room, or clear something in your heart — and offer it to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A lone figure standing in a vast, golden desert, arms raised, mouth open, calling out across the empty land. Wind moves his clothing. In the distance, a winding path leads toward a bright horizon. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
verses.little_bible: The men who had been sent to question John were Pharisees — strict religious teachers.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The men questioning John were religious teachers called Pharisees.
verses.meaning: The Pharisees were the most influential religious group, known for strict rule-keeping. Their interrogation of John was official and loaded with authority.
verses.memory_phrase: Religious leaders questioned John.
verses.prayer: God, help me not be like the Pharisees who asked questions to argue, but someone who asks questions to learn.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think religious teachers would want to question John? Were they looking for the truth, or just testing him?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes people who know a lot about religion are not close to God. How does your family make sure your faith is real and personal — not just rules?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God one honest question today — and then listen quietly for a moment.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of men in fine, detailed religious robes with tassels and headcoverings, arms folded, observing John from a distance with calculating expressions. Contrast with John's simple dress. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And they asked him, and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not that Christ, nor Elias, neither that prophet?
verses.little_bible: They challenged him: 'If you are not the Messiah, or Elijah, or the prophet — then why are you baptising people? What gives you the right?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They asked: 'If you are not the Christ, why are you baptising people?'
verses.meaning: Baptism was associated with preparing people for the Messiah's arrival. If John was not the Messiah or a prophet, his authority to baptise was questioned.
verses.memory_phrase: John baptised to prepare for Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, help me do the things You call me to do, even when people question me or ask 'by what right?'
verses.discussion_question: The Pharisees questioned John's authority. Have you ever been told you could not do something that you knew God had asked you to do?
verses.family_discussion: John had authority from God, not from men. How does your family discern when to follow human authority and when to follow God's authority?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing you feel called to do for God. Do it today — even if it seems small.
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing knee-deep in a river while official-looking men on the bank point and gesture with scrolls, questioning him. John looks confident and at peace. Bright river scene, watercolour.

verses.kjv: John answered them, saying, I baptize with water: but there standeth one among you, whom ye know not;
verses.little_bible: John replied: 'I baptise with water. But right now, someone is standing among you — and you do not even know who He is.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I use water. But someone is standing with you right now — and you do not know Him.'
verses.meaning: John's water baptism was preparation. Jesus — the one they could not see — was already present among them. The Messiah was in their midst unrecognised.
verses.memory_phrase: Someone stands among you whom you do not know.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me recognise Your presence with me today. You are always near, even when I do not notice.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus was right there among the people and they did not know it! How can we become better at noticing where Jesus is at work around us?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is present with us all the time. How does your family practise awareness of Jesus's presence throughout the day?
verses.do_it_today: Three times today, pause and say quietly: 'Jesus, You are here with me right now.'
verses.illustration_prompt: John in the river, gesturing toward the crowd on the bank. Among the crowd, one figure — Jesus — stands peacefully, almost glowing faintly. The crowd around Him does not notice. Watercolour, natural light.

verses.kjv: He it is, who coming after me is preferred before me, whose shoe's latchet I am not worthy to unloose.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'He is the one coming after me — and He is so much greater than I am that I am not even worthy to undo His sandals.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'Jesus is so great, I am not even worthy to untie His sandals.'
verses.meaning: Untying sandals was a servant's task. John said he was not even worthy to be Jesus's servant. This is deep, joyful humility — not shame, but awe.
verses.memory_phrase: I am not worthy to untie His sandal.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are so great and so holy. I am amazed that You love me. Thank You for coming near to me.
verses.discussion_question: John said he was not even worthy to be Jesus's sandal-untier. Why do you think he felt that way — and yet still served Jesus happily?
verses.family_discussion: True humility before God is not self-hatred but a clear view of how great He is. How does your family cultivate genuine awe of Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of service for someone else today — something small and unseen — as a way of honouring Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in simple clothing bowing low near a pair of dusty sandals, with a sense of reverence and peace — not fear. Bright outdoor light, golden dust, the Jordan river in the background. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.
verses.little_bible: All of this happened at a place called Bethabara, on the other side of the Jordan River, where John was baptising people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This all happened by the Jordan River where John was baptising.
verses.meaning: John's gospel is careful with geography. This was a real place — a real river — where real events happened. The gospel is grounded in history.
verses.memory_phrase: Real events at a real place.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Jesus's story is real — not a fairy tale. It happened in real places with real people.
verses.discussion_question: Why does it matter that John tells us exactly where these things happened?
verses.family_discussion: Our faith is based on real historical events. How does knowing that Jesus actually walked on earth — in real places — strengthen your faith?
verses.do_it_today: Look up where Israel and the Jordan River are on a map. Picture John there, baptising people.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wide, slow-moving river (the Jordan) with reed-lined banks and golden afternoon light. People wading in the water while John baptises them. Distant hills in the background. Peaceful watercolour scene.

verses.kjv: The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.
verses.little_bible: The next day John saw Jesus walking toward him. He called out: 'Look! There is the Lamb of God — the one who takes away all the sin of the world!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John saw Jesus and said: 'Look! The Lamb of God who takes away sin!'
verses.meaning: This is one of the most important announcements in the Bible. Jesus is identified as the sacrificial Lamb whose death will remove the sin of all people.
verses.memory_phrase: Behold the Lamb of God!
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Lamb of God. Thank You for taking away my sin. I am forgiven because of You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think John called Jesus a 'Lamb'? What did lambs mean in Bible times?
verses.family_discussion: In the Old Testament, lambs were sacrificed to cover sin. Jesus is the final, perfect Lamb. How does this truth change the way your family thinks about forgiveness?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a lamb and write next to it: 'Jesus takes away my sin.' Put it somewhere you will see it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing by the river, pointing with outstretched arm toward Jesus walking along the bank. Jesus is calm and unhurried. Sunlight breaks through the clouds above him. Warm, sacred watercolour moment.

verses.kjv: This is he of whom I said, After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: for he was before me.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'This is the one I told you about — He comes after me in time, but He is far greater than me, because He existed long before I was born.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'This is the one I told you about. He is greater because He was before me.'
verses.meaning: John repeats his key testimony: Jesus is greater because He is eternal. John came before in ministry but after in birth — yet Jesus preceded him in eternity.
verses.memory_phrase: He comes after — but He was before.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are eternal and You are greater than everyone. I worship You!
verses.discussion_question: How can someone come 'after' in time but be 'before' in importance? What makes Jesus different?
verses.family_discussion: John was not confused or threatened by Jesus's greatness. He celebrated it. How can your family cultivate that attitude — genuinely celebrating those who are greater?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate someone in your family today — say something specific and wonderful about them.
verses.illustration_prompt: John gesturing toward Jesus with one hand on his own chest and the other pointing outward. His face is full of joyful certainty. The crowd listens intently. River scene, bright sky, watercolour.

verses.kjv: And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel, therefore am I come baptizing with water.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'I did not know who He was at first — but God sent me to baptise with water so that Jesus would be revealed to Israel.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I did not know Him at first. I baptised to help reveal Him to Israel.'
verses.meaning: John's ministry was divinely appointed to prepare and reveal. Even he needed God to show him who Jesus was. Revelation is always a gift from God.
verses.memory_phrase: God reveals Jesus to those who seek.
verses.prayer: God, reveal Jesus to me more and more. Open my eyes to see who He truly is.
verses.discussion_question: John did not recognise Jesus at first. How does God help us recognise Jesus and understand who He is?
verses.family_discussion: Knowing Jesus is a revelation from God — not just information we collect. How has God revealed Jesus to your family in a personal, real way?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to reveal something new about Jesus to you today. Then read one verse from the Gospels and listen.
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing in the river with his arm raised in mid-baptism, pausing, a thoughtful and wondering expression on his face. Dawn light breaking over the hills. Gentle watercolour glow.

verses.kjv: And John bare record, saying, I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it abode upon him.
verses.little_bible: John told everyone what he had seen: 'I watched the Holy Spirit come down from heaven like a dove and rest on Jesus — and stay there.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I saw the Holy Spirit come down like a dove and rest on Jesus.'
verses.meaning: This is John's eyewitness testimony of Jesus's baptism. The dove symbolises peace and the Holy Spirit's presence. God confirmed Jesus publicly.
verses.memory_phrase: The Spirit came like a dove on Jesus.
verses.prayer: Holy Spirit, rest on me too. Come and stay, like You stayed on Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the Holy Spirit came down like a dove and not something else — like an eagle or a lion?
verses.family_discussion: The Holy Spirit rested on and remained with Jesus. How does your family experience the Holy Spirit's presence? Do you invite Him to stay?
verses.do_it_today: Sit quietly for two minutes today and invite the Holy Spirit: 'Come and rest on me, Holy Spirit. Stay.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A white dove descending from a golden sky, wings spread, settling gently onto the shoulder of Jesus standing in a river. Sunlight breaking through clouds, water rippling. Tender, sacred watercolour moment.

verses.kjv: And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'I did not know who Jesus was until God told me: the one you see the Spirit rest on and stay on — that is the one who will baptise people with the Holy Spirit.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God told John: 'The one the Spirit rests on is the one who baptises with the Holy Spirit.'
verses.meaning: God gave John a specific sign to identify the Messiah. Jesus's baptism was divinely confirmed. And Jesus's baptism is greater — He gives the Holy Spirit Himself.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus baptises with the Holy Spirit.
verses.prayer: Jesus, baptise me with Your Holy Spirit. Fill me and let Your Spirit rest on me too.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between being baptised with water and being baptised with the Holy Spirit?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gives the Holy Spirit to those who follow Him. Has your family prayed together to receive more of the Holy Spirit? How has He been at work in your home?
verses.do_it_today: As a family, pray: 'Jesus, we ask You to baptise us afresh with the Holy Spirit today.'
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing at the river's edge, eyes raised toward heaven with a look of sudden recognition. A dove is visible above Jesus in the water. Golden light, deep blue water, watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And I saw, and bare record that this is the Son of God.
verses.little_bible: John said: 'I saw it with my own eyes — and I tell everyone: Jesus is the Son of God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John said: 'I saw it. Jesus is the Son of God.'
verses.meaning: This is John's core declaration: personal witness leading to public proclamation. He saw, he believed, and he testified. Faith and testimony go together.
verses.memory_phrase: I saw it — Jesus is the Son of God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are the Son of God. I want to tell others what I know about You.
verses.discussion_question: John said 'I saw it.' What have YOU seen or experienced that shows you Jesus is real?
verses.family_discussion: John was an eyewitness. We testify from experience. As a family, share one moment when each person knew — really knew — that Jesus is real.
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something you personally know or believe about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: John standing tall on the riverbank, hand raised, face radiant, calling out to a gathered crowd. Above the river, a lingering golden light marks where the Spirit descended. Joyful, confident. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;
verses.little_bible: The next day, John was standing there again with two of his disciples — two people who were following him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The next day John stood with two of his followers.
verses.meaning: The scene is set simply. Two disciples stand with their teacher John — about to have their lives changed by meeting Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Two disciples stood with John.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to be one of Your disciples. I want to follow You.
verses.discussion_question: What is a disciple? Would you like to be a disciple of Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: These two men were about to meet Jesus and become His followers. When did each family member decide to follow Jesus — or when do they hope to?
verses.do_it_today: Write your name down and next to it write: 'Disciple of Jesus.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Three figures standing on a riverside path in morning light — John and two younger men beside him. They look forward together, alert, as if about to see something. Calm, expectant. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!
verses.little_bible: John looked up and saw Jesus walking by. He said again: 'Look! There is the Lamb of God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John saw Jesus walking and said: 'Look! The Lamb of God!'
verses.meaning: John pointed to Jesus a second time. His message was consistent: it was always about Jesus, never about himself. This pointing led his own disciples to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: John kept pointing to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want my life to keep pointing to You — again and again.
verses.discussion_question: John pointed others to Jesus even though it meant his own disciples might leave him and follow Jesus. What does that tell you about John?
verses.family_discussion: The best thing John could do for his disciples was point them to Jesus. How does your family point each other — and others — to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Point a friend toward something Jesus said today — share a verse or a story with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: John pointing at a walking figure of Jesus in the distance along a path. Two younger men beside John turn their heads to follow his gaze. Morning light, river path, watercolour.

verses.kjv: And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
verses.little_bible: When the two disciples heard John say that, they left and began following Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The two disciples heard John and followed Jesus.
verses.meaning: John's testimony did its job. The disciples followed Jesus — which was the whole point of John's ministry. True witness leads people to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: They heard — and they followed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I hear Your call, help me follow — just like those two disciples did.
verses.discussion_question: These disciples heard one sentence from John and decided to follow Jesus. What made them willing to follow so quickly?
verses.family_discussion: Following Jesus starts with hearing about Him. Who first pointed your family to Jesus? How can you pass on what you received?
verses.do_it_today: Thank someone today who helped you believe in or learn about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two young men walking quickly and eagerly along a dusty path behind a distant figure of Jesus. Their steps are light and purposeful. Blue sky, morning light, watercolour style.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?
verses.little_bible: Jesus turned around and saw them following. He asked them: 'What are you looking for?' They called Him 'Rabbi' — which means Teacher — and asked: 'Where are You staying?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus turned and asked: 'What are you looking for?' They asked: 'Teacher, where do You live?'
verses.meaning: 'What do you seek?' is Jesus's first recorded question in John's gospel. It is a question He asks every person. What are we truly looking for in life?
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus asks: What are you looking for?
verses.prayer: Jesus, I am looking for You. You are what I truly need. Show me where You are.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus asked: 'What are you looking for?' If He asked you that today, what would you say?
verses.family_discussion: This is the first question Jesus asks in John's gospel. How does your family answer this question: what are you really seeking in life?
verses.do_it_today: Write down your honest answer to Jesus's question: 'What are you looking for?' Then talk to Him about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turned around on a path, looking kindly at two young men who have stopped in their tracks. His expression is warm, inviting, genuinely curious. Dusty golden path, morning light, watercolour.

verses.kjv: He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said simply: 'Come and see.' So they went with Him, saw where He was staying, and spent the whole rest of that day with Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Come and see.' So they went and spent the whole day with Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus's invitation is 'Come and see' — not 'study this first' or 'earn your way.' He welcomes the curious. Spending time with Jesus changes everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Come and see.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I accept Your invitation. I am coming to see — to know You more.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said 'Come and see' — He did not say 'Come if you are good enough.' What does that tell you about how Jesus welcomes people?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples spent the whole day with Jesus. How does your family spend time in Jesus's presence — not just praying quickly, but really being with Him?
verses.do_it_today: Set aside 10 minutes today just to be with Jesus — not to pray a list, but to sit quietly and know He is with you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus extending a hand toward two young men, gesturing warmly down a path toward a simple dwelling. His face is open and welcoming. Afternoon golden light, warm watercolour tones.

verses.kjv: One of the two which heard John speak, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.
verses.little_bible: One of the two men who had followed Jesus was Andrew — the brother of Simon Peter.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: One of the two men was Andrew, Peter's brother.
verses.meaning: Andrew is named here. He is notable not for leading thousands but for bringing his brother Peter to Jesus. Small acts of witness have enormous consequences.
verses.memory_phrase: Andrew met Jesus first.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me bring the people I love to You — just like Andrew brought his brother.
verses.discussion_question: Andrew found Jesus and then immediately thought of his brother. Who do YOU most want to bring to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Andrew's greatest contribution was bringing Peter to Jesus — and Peter became the leader of the early church. How does your family think about the ripple effect of simple witness?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one family member or friend who does not yet know Jesus. Pray for them by name today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man (Andrew) walking quickly and excitedly along a village path, turning his head back as if about to call to someone. Houses and trees around him. Bright midday light, watercolour style.

verses.kjv: He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.
verses.little_bible: Andrew ran straight to his brother Simon and told him: 'We have found the Messiah!' — which means the promised King, the Christ.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Andrew found his brother Simon and said: 'We have found the Messiah — the Christ!'
verses.meaning: Andrew's first instinct after meeting Jesus was to find his brother. The discovery of Jesus naturally overflows into telling others. This is the essence of witness.
verses.memory_phrase: We have found the Messiah!
verses.prayer: Jesus, the excitement Andrew felt — let me feel that too! Help me want to tell others that I have found You.
verses.discussion_question: Andrew was so excited that he ran to tell his brother straight away. How do you feel when you have amazing news to share?
verses.family_discussion: When did your family first 'find' Jesus — or realise He had found you? Who did you tell first?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today: 'I have found something amazing — let me tell you about Jesus.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man grabbing his brother by the shoulders with an enormous excited grin, eyes shining. The brother looks surprised but intrigued. A sunny village square, children playing nearby. Joyful watercolour scene.

verses.kjv: And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.
verses.little_bible: Andrew brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said: 'You are Simon, son of Jonah. But I am going to call you Cephas — which means Rock.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Andrew brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus said: 'You will be called Rock.'
verses.meaning: Jesus looked at Simon and saw who he would become. He gave him a new name — Peter (Rock) — a name that described his future, not his present failures.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus saw who Peter would become.
verses.prayer: Jesus, look at me too. See who You made me to be — and help me become that person.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus gave Simon a new name that described his future. If Jesus gave you a new name about your future, what do you think it might be?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus called Simon by his future — Peter, the Rock — before Peter had done anything to earn it. How does God's view of your future change how you parent or how you see yourself?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God: 'What do You see in me?' Sit quietly and listen. Write down any thoughts that come.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly and warmly into the eyes of a broad-shouldered fisherman who looks surprised and moved. Jesus's hand rests on the man's shoulder. Golden late afternoon light, watercolour style.

verses.kjv: The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.
verses.little_bible: The next day, Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said two words: 'Follow Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus found Philip and said: 'Follow Me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus takes the initiative. He found Philip — not the other way around. His call is direct, personal, and powerful: two words that changed a life.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus said: Follow Me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I hear You saying 'Follow Me.' I say yes. I will follow You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus just said two words — 'Follow Me' — and Philip followed. What do you think made Jesus so compelling?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus calls people personally and directly. How has each family member heard Jesus's personal call to follow Him? How did you respond?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud today: 'Jesus, I follow You.' Mean it. Let it be your commitment for today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a sunlit path in Galilee, looking over his shoulder at a young man (Philip) with a warm, inviting expression. Philip is mid-step, caught between where he was going and where Jesus is heading. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
verses.little_bible: Philip was from Bethsaida — the same town where Andrew and Peter lived.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Philip was from the same town as Andrew and Peter.
verses.meaning: Geography matters. These men were neighbours. Jesus was building a community from the same place. God works through real communities and relationships.
verses.memory_phrase: They were neighbours called by Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for the community You placed me in. Help me see the people near me as people You love.
verses.discussion_question: Philip, Andrew, and Peter were from the same town. Do you think God uses the neighbourhood or place where you live as part of His plan?
verses.family_discussion: God placed these disciples near each other intentionally. How has God used your neighbourhood, school, or community to build His kingdom?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one neighbour by name today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small fishing village on the edge of a lake — simple stone houses, boats tied up, fishing nets drying. Three young men chatting and laughing outside. Blue sky and sparkling water. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.
verses.little_bible: Philip found his friend Nathanael and told him excitedly: 'We have found the one Moses and the prophets wrote about — Jesus, from Nazareth, the son of Joseph!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Philip told his friend Nathanael: 'We found the one the prophets wrote about — Jesus of Nazareth!'
verses.meaning: Philip connected Jesus to all of Scripture. The Old Testament pointed forward to Jesus. Recognising this is a key part of understanding who Jesus is.
verses.memory_phrase: The Scriptures point to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, the whole Bible is about You. Help me see You on every page.
verses.discussion_question: Philip said Moses and the prophets wrote about Jesus long before He was born. Does it surprise you that the whole Bible is about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The Old Testament pointed to Jesus. How does reading the Old Testament help your family understand Jesus better? Can you think of a story that connects?
verses.do_it_today: Open to any page of the Old Testament and ask: 'How does this point to Jesus?' Try it together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two young men in animated conversation. One (Philip) is holding open a scroll and pointing at it enthusiastically. The other (Nathanael) leans in with interest. Sunset, golden watercolour tones.

verses.kjv: And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.
verses.little_bible: Nathanael raised an eyebrow: 'Nazareth? Can anything good really come from there?' Philip just smiled and said: 'Come and see.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nathanael said: 'Anything good from Nazareth?' Philip said: 'Come and see.'
verses.meaning: Nathanael's doubt was real. Nazareth was a small, unremarkable town. Philip did not argue — he invited. The invitation 'Come and see' is always the right response to doubt.
verses.memory_phrase: When you doubt, come and see.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I have doubts, help me not to stay away but to come closer and see for myself.
verses.discussion_question: Nathanael doubted before he even met Jesus. Have you ever doubted something before you tried it? What happened when you gave it a chance?
verses.family_discussion: Philip's answer to doubt was not an argument — it was an invitation. How does your family handle doubt and questions about faith?
verses.do_it_today: If you have a question or doubt about God today, write it down and bring it to Jesus. Say: 'Jesus, I want to come and see.'
verses.illustration_prompt: One young man with a sceptical, amused expression (Nathanael) and another (Philip) grinning and gesturing ahead down a path with confident warmth. Afternoon light, village backdrop, watercolour.

verses.kjv: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!
verses.little_bible: When Jesus saw Nathanael walking toward Him, He said: 'Here is a true Israelite — a man with nothing false or tricky about him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw Nathanael and said: 'Here is an honest man — truly!'
verses.meaning: Jesus saw Nathanael's character before they even spoke. Jesus sees us as we truly are — and loves what is genuine. Nathanael's honesty (even in doubt) was commendable.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sees who you truly are.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You see my heart completely. Thank You that You love me anyway.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus could see what Nathanael was like inside before he even said a word. How does it feel knowing Jesus can see your heart?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus saw Nathanael's honesty as something precious. How does your family value honesty — even when it comes in the form of doubt or hard questions?
verses.do_it_today: Be completely honest with God today — tell Him exactly how you feel, even if it is complicated.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing at the edge of a path, smiling warmly as Nathanael approaches with a slightly unsure expression. Jesus looks at him with knowing, gentle eyes. Warm afternoon light, watercolour.

verses.kjv: Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.
verses.little_bible: Nathanael was astonished: 'How do You know me?' Jesus answered: 'Before Philip even called you — when you were sitting under the fig tree — I saw you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nathanael asked: 'How do You know me?' Jesus said: 'I saw you under the fig tree before Philip called you.'
verses.meaning: Jesus's knowledge of Nathanael was intimate and supernatural. This is the moment Nathanael's doubt crumbled — Jesus knew him privately, completely.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus saw you before anyone called you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You saw me before I even knew about You. You have always known me. That is amazing.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus saw Nathanael when no one else was watching. Does it comfort you to know Jesus sees you even in private, hidden moments?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew Nathanael completely — in the quiet, private moment under the fig tree. How does knowing God sees every part of your life change how you live day to day?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a quiet moment today — eating, walking, alone — and remember: Jesus sees you right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: Nathanael sitting alone under a large fig tree, dappled light through the leaves, in a peaceful moment of thought. In the sky above, a soft warm light suggests being watched over with love. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel.
verses.little_bible: Nathanael was overwhelmed. He said: 'Teacher — You are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nathanael said: 'You are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!'
verses.meaning: From sceptic to worshipper in one moment. Nathanael's encounter with Jesus's knowledge of him produced instant, wholehearted faith. Being known by Jesus leads to worship.
verses.memory_phrase: You are the Son of God!
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Son of God and the King of everything. I worship You!
verses.discussion_question: Nathanael went from 'Can anything good come from Nazareth?' to 'You are the Son of God!' very fast. What changed?
verses.family_discussion: Nathanael's faith came from a personal encounter with Jesus who knew him. How does personal experience of Jesus's knowledge of us deepen our faith?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud — with real feeling: 'Jesus, You are the Son of God. You are my King.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Nathanael on his knees before Jesus, eyes wide with wonder and joy, hands open. Jesus smiles gently down at him. Warm golden light, open countryside behind them. Glorious, tender watercolour moment.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.
verses.little_bible: Jesus smiled and said to him: 'You believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You have not seen anything yet — even greater things are coming!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You believe because of that? You will see even greater things!'
verses.meaning: Jesus affirms Nathanael's faith but points forward — there is always more with Jesus. His followers will see greater signs and wonders. Following Jesus is a journey of increasing revelation.
verses.memory_phrase: You will see greater things!
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You now — and I am excited to see the greater things You have for me!
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said 'greater things are coming.' What do you think some of those greater things could be?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told Nathanael that what he had just experienced was only the beginning. How does your family hold onto a sense of expectation and wonder in your walk with God?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing you are expecting God to do — one 'greater thing' — and pray about it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus raising a finger with a joyful, knowing smile, as if saying 'just you wait.' Nathanael looks up at Him, face full of anticipation and delight. Open sky above them, horizon wide. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said solemnly: 'I tell you the truth — one day you will see heaven open wide, and the angels of God going up and down on the Son of Man.' Jesus was talking about Himself.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You will see heaven open and angels going up and down on the Son of Man.'
verses.meaning: Jesus references Jacob's ladder (Genesis 28) — He is the ladder between heaven and earth. The way to God is through Him. Heaven is open because of Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Heaven is open through Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the way to God. Because of You, heaven is open to me. Thank You!
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said heaven is open and the angels go up and down through Him. What does it mean that Jesus is the connection between us and God?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is the ladder between heaven and earth — the only way to God. How does your family communicate this truth to each other and to others?
verses.do_it_today: Read the story of Jacob's ladder in Genesis 28:10-17 and then say: 'Jesus, You ARE the ladder. You connect me to God.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A vast open sky with a beam of brilliant golden light descending from heaven to where Jesus stands, with angels ascending and descending on the light beam. Jesus stands at the base, arms wide. Majestic, tender watercolour scene.
